Title: The OsmiRNA166b-OsHox32 pair regulates mechanical strength of rice plants by modulating cell wall biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell walls of leaves and culms, especially sclerenchyma cells around vascular bundles and epidermal/vascular tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides cell wall thickness and mechanical strength of rice leaves and culms
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of OsmiR166b increases cellulose and lignin accumulation, thickens cell walls, and enhances culm mechanical strength by repressing OsHox32; knockdown causes reduced cellulose/lignin and brittle culms
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: STTM knockdown; overexpression of OsMIR166b; qRT-PCR; target-site mutation test by RT-PCR; cellulose/lignin quantification; histochemical staining; SEM/TEM
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported to be differentially expressed in different tissues of rice; specific pattern not detailed in this paper
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed mainly in leaves at tillering stage in transgenic rice
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Paddy field under natural conditions during summer and autumn seasons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsmiR166b cleaves OsHox32 transcript. 2. Overexpression of OsmiR166b phenocopies OsHox32 knockdown, causing increased cellulose and lignin accumulation and thicker cell walls. 3. Knockdown of OsmiR166b causes drooping leaves and brittle culms associated with reduced cellulose and lignin.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory module for improving culm strength and lodging resistance by elevating miR166b activity or weakening OsHox32, but potential effects on plant architecture and development should be monitored
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced miR166b activity caused rolled/drooping leaves, shorter internodes, reduced plant height, and brittle culms; broader developmental effects are likely because miR166b regulates HD-ZIP III genes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reduction of hydroxycinnamyl aldehydes to their corresponding alcohols in the last step of monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate monolignol precursors of lignin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/major determinant step because the paper states OsCAD2 is the bona fide CAD gene playing the major role in monolignol biosynthesis and a key switch controlling lignin content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secondary cell walls of leaves and culms, especially vascular bundle and supporting tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides rigidity and resistance to compressive force, contributing to mechanical strength
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher OsCAD2 expression is associated with increased lignin accumulation and stronger cell walls; repression by OsHox32 lowers lignin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in transgenic lines; yeast one-hybrid; ChIP-PCR; dual-luciferase assay for transcriptional repression by OsHox32 and co-suppression with OSH15
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in leaves of transgenic plants at tillering stage; significantly increased in OE166b/RNAiHox32 and decreased in STTM166b/OEHox32
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCAD2 transcript levels positively correlate with lignin accumulation in OsmiR166b/OsHox32 transgenic plants. 2. OsHox32 directly binds OsCAD2 promoter and suppresses its expression. 3. Co-expression of OsHox32 with OSH15 synergistically enhances repression of OsCAD2.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsCAD2 promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong leverage point for tuning lignin content and culm strength; repression may soften biomass but risks weakening stems, whereas maintaining expression supports structural robustness
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced OsCAD2 expression is linked to lower lignin and brittle culms; excessive suppression may impair plant mechanical support
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats OsCAD2 as the bona fide major CAD in rice culm lignification

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HD-ZIP III family; START domain-containing homeobox protein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell walls of leaves and culms
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall structural support and mechanical strength
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of cellulose, lignin, and related cell wall polymers
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses OsCAD2 and OsCESA7 directly; expression of OsCESA6, OsCSLF6 and Os4CL3 is positively associated with OsHox32 loss and negatively associated with OsHox32 overexpression, but direct regulation was not shown
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A major negative regulator linking miR166b to reduced lignin and cellulose biosynthetic gene expression and weakened cell walls
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with OSH15; OsHox32-OSH15 complex synergistically represses OsCAD2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsHox32 is a bona fide target of OsmiR166b. 2. Overexpression of OsHox32 reduces cellulose and lignin content, decreases cell wall thickness, and causes drooping leaves and brittle culms. 3. OsHox32 directly binds OsCAD2 and OsCESA7 promoters to repress their expression. 4. OsHox32 interacts with OSH15 through its START domain and together they synergistically repress OsCAD2.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of cellulose and lignin accumulation, reducing mechanical strength when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi in rice; qRT-PCR; Y1H; ChIP-PCR; Y2H; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; cellulose/lignin measurements; SEM/TEM
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Manipulated and assayed mainly in leaves at tillering stage; transgene effects observed in leaves and culms
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; RNAi construct for suppression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation of OsHox32 could be used to enhance culm strength and lodging resistance by increasing cellulose/lignin deposition; avoid overexpression because it weakens stems and alters plant architecture
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes brittle culms, drooping/rolled leaves, shorter internodes, and reduced plant height; broader developmental perturbation due to HD-ZIP III function
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The START domain harbors the miR166b cleavage site and is required for interaction with OSH15

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secondary cell walls of internodes/culms and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports wall rigidity and mechanical strength
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses OsCAD2; synergistically enhances OsHox32-mediated repression of OsCAD2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important co-repressor of a key lignin biosynthetic gene, OsCAD2
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms an interaction complex with OsHox32
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OSH15 physically interacts with OsHox32. 2. Co-expression of OSH15 and OsHox32 synergistically suppresses OsCAD2 expression. 3. The interaction does not further alter OsHox32 suppression of OsCESA7.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of lignin biosynthesis through enhanced suppression of OsCAD2 when partnered with OsHox32
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial target with OsHox32 for modulating lignin and stem strength, but suppression of lignin may compromise culm robustness
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Negative regulation of lignin can reduce mechanical strength if overly active
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because it is experimentally interrogated in the Results as an interacting co-regulator affecting lignin gene repression
